Race: ALL

Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

# Nov 27 2001 at 7:46 PM Rating: Decent

this robe look awesome...look like the robe of living fungus...i would just buy/camp this for the look and plus the stats are not too bad...

prexus

RE: price
QuoteReply
# Feb 13 2003 at 6:16 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592756961840
49 posts
The robe, while similar, doesn't look like the Robe of the Living Fungus.

This one is practically all green, while Robe of the Living Fungus is green with lots of brownish tints over it, and a different symbol design instead of the sun.

RE: price

# Dec 07 2001 at 12:55 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247364644
10 posts
what's a CCS and what does it cost?
▲ Page top
RE: price
QuoteReply
# May 08 2002 at 12:27 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591775089716
6 posts
CCS= Crystal Covered Shroud, it drops off a mob in CC. I cant exactly remember stats off hand but I believe it is 12ac 4str/wis/int and has a save on it and weights 1.0
▲ Page top
RE: price
QuoteReply
# Sep 28 2002 at 10:19 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255447530
19 posts
yes the stats are correct like he said but ive seen them go from 75 to 100pp, buti guess im lucky, i got one for my monk for 50pp, along with a sap of piety for 50pp too )
▲ Page top
Not so great for monks
QuoteReply
# Nov 10 2001 at 3:48 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770492772
6 posts
If you're a monk, you're better off buying a CCS. Much better stats, and I've seen them go as low as 200pp.
▲ Page top
RE: Not so great for monks
QuoteReply
# Sep 15 2002 at 1:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766675380
Scholar
222 posts
Actually, at least on 7th hammer they've gone as low as 70p in the bazaar. It seems like the most pharmed items have also (not suprisingly) become worth a lot less since the advent of being able to search for an item and see that 24 people are selling something, like CCS or my favorite the Steel Polished Ulak. (buy from a vendor for 3-5 plat, fairly common drop off a midlevel mob.... result? Hundreds for sale in the bazaar, some for 50, some for 5)

Maybe I'm exagerating a bit, but search for "polished" sometime in the bazaar, I bet you;ll see what I mean.
▲ Page top
Why i use this robe
QuoteReply
# Aug 18 2001 at 1:31 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361547059
25 posts
I use this robe for a few reasons, first im a erudite wizard. My int is already high and without the robe its 205..so that doesnt really matter. I know the 6 AC is low, but 3 agility is nice..since agility is needed so you dont get hit as much. 3 str is nice...considering the fact im using a -8 str ring (dont laugh..ring gives me 8 int, 45 mana, and other good stuff). As for the mana...15 is decent. Then theres the look...this robe kicks *** in that part cause no one has it...i get tired of having the same robe 5 other ppl in the zone have. Anyway..thats my opinion.

Fauzen Majere
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 12 2001 at 2:30 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Im selling this robe on Druzzil ro if anyone is interested. Give me a tell.
oracle?
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2001 at 9:32 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591759868519
11 posts
is this robe better that the robe of the oracle?
▲ Page top
RE: oracle?
QuoteReply
# Apr 21 2002 at 1:12 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1717373788612
View User Forum
Scholar
485 posts
No until you go on raids AC is king besides as a monk i would rather have the CCS 12AC is much better even if it is worn by a lot of people it is still good to have
▲ Page top
RE: oracle?
QuoteReply
# Aug 13 2001 at 8:37 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750644231
44 posts
In my opinion, this isn't as good as the Robe of the Oracle for the Int Casters, but may be better for monks.

The Robe of the Oracle has the following stats:
AC: +9 Wis: +5 Int: +5 Mana: +25

This robe has a lower AC, lower Int and lower Mana. But, this robe has 3 STR and 3 AGI, which can be useful for a monk. It does look much better than the Robe of the Oracle (it is a dark green color, compared to light blue with yellow pasties), but looks don't save your butt.

Sunil Grubsov
Iksar Necro (level 23 Occultist)
Vazaelle
▲ Page top
Dalnir Robe
QuoteReply
# Jul 28 2001 at 2:39 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761841954
2 posts
I have looted two of these robes so far...gave one to a monk and one to my wizard...fortunately stats aren't everything to me...

First time I got a robe I was with a lvl50 nec guardian...he killed the Imp once and dropped the robe first try, then I put it on my monk then went back there after I got 46, was with a 39 Monk friend, he went in there and got utterly fried to near death, if casters are up...make that if the Imp is up and your a 39 monk your going to get screwd unless you have a Fungi Tunic and some Uber gear. I ST'd the imp and sent my pet and my monk friend on the Invoker...killed it quick with Ignite bones, killed the Gnome Mage then killed the Imp, camped the Imp til he dropped Robe (took bout 10 spawns)
▲ Page top
Drop
QuoteReply
# Jun 03 2001 at 7:18 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I was there at lvl27 on my Necromancer and had a lvl50 necromancer guarding me trying to get some Grave Sandals, and Worm Eaten gloves and we were just clearing out down there and that Kly dropped the Robe the first time we killed it ;)
▲ Page top
Compare this...
QuoteReply
# May 14 2001 at 8:47 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Compare this... The Robe of Dalnir is AC6, +3 INT, and +15 mana. The Robe of the Oracle has AC9, +5 INT and +25 mana.

The only reason to have this robe for casters is for looks.

If you're a monk, it's a great robe I'm told. I'm not sure what other robes monks can wear beside this and the Oracle robe.

just got to dalnir, and while i'm shopping with my monk, i thought i'd pick up some stuff for my necro, as for this being worth 250pp, i doubt it, as was said below there are a lot better robes out there, and i think my necro will be keeping his green silk drape for a while longer ;)
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 25 2001 at 10:24 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I wouldnt pay more then 250pp for that robe, who cares what it looks like and how hard it is to get, it is only 6 ac, hell there are plenty of chest pieces that are alot better and abit cheaper

# May 14 2001 at 8:52 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The Robe of the Oracle and the Green Silken Drape (arguably) are both better than this robe. And alot cheaper.

Oracle: AC9, +5 INT, +25 mana
GSD: AC8, +9 DEX, +9 STA, +3 INT, SV POISON +15

#Anonymous, Posted: May 18 2001 at 12:28 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) but those two robes look like ***

# May 24 2001 at 11:01 AM Rating: Default

View User Forum
Anonymous
Regardless, he's right. Those two robes are better for caster stat wise. If you into looks, go for the leather look of the white scale tunic.

And besides, the poster who was heckling the first one is flat out wrong. He states there aren't any better robes. He's obviously quite clueless.

WOW!
QuoteReply
# Apr 13 2001 at 2:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1685924205458
39 posts
I got totally lucky. I broke the room (almost had to gate, but got a stun through then rooted). Summoned my L49 pet to keep it cleared. 20 minutes after I broke the room the Imp pops dies in seconds (Enc pets rule btw, I dont care what anyone says) and he dropped the robe! I was Level 52 enchanter, just collecting robes. Since I was already there on my epic, figured what the heck, I'd camp it a bit. WOW
▲ Page top
RE: WOW!
QuoteReply
# May 29 2001 at 1:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246654228
6 posts
WOW, you lucky ******* hehe. I haven't even been able to clear the room at 46. The foci room is so much easier my pet owned it by itself. I want one of these really bad, it would look so tight on my iksar monk. (Enchanter pets suck, my 44 pet would probally own yours)

Deepsky 46 Mage

▲ Page top
chanter pets
QuoteReply
# Jul 05 2001 at 8:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246113205
28 posts
lol. your 44 pet WOULD own, hands down, chanter pets are the sorriest excuse for an excuse that ever was. chanter pets are good for nothing but farmin greenies. any mob of equal level will school a chanter pet in exactly 2.7 seconds, so why bother. chanter pets are a minor distraction used to give you a 3 second lead on a mob when you book for the zone :)

#Anonymous, Posted: Dec 10 2000 at 10:44 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) this robe drops off of Kly Imprecator in dalnirs crypt of course
